analysis_result: Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 (non-syndromic spinal NF methylation subgroup) has the highest CIBERSORTx-like score for the non-syndromic spinal NF (NF Cell Type) signature. Based on marker-based approximation (paper's custom CIBERSORTx signature not available): canonical Schwann-cell markers give mean z-score 0.501 (G5) vs 0.230 (G3), 0.186 (G4), -0.409 (G2), -1.313 (G1); data-driven G5-enriched markers give 0.804 (G5) vs 0.257 (G4), 0.098 (G3), -0.830 (G2), -1.056 (G1). G5 is highest in both approaches. Note: Only 5/7 methylation subgroups have bulk RNA-seq data (G6 and G7 cutaneous NF groups have zero bulk samples).
denominator_used: N = 48 bulk RNA-seq samples (MPNST_G1=6, MPNST_G2=6, PremalignantNF_G3=18, Benign_NF-G4=9, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=9; Cutaneous NF_G6 and G7 have no bulk samples)
assumptions_made: Paper's custom CIBERSORTx signature matrix is unavailable, so used marker-based module-score approximation instead; 'non-syndromic spinal NF' = Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 methylation group; canonical Schwann/peripheral-nerve markers serve as a reasonable proxy for the 'NF Cell Type' signature; only 5 of the 7 methylation subgroups can be evaluated because G6 and G7 (cutaneous NF) have no bulk RNA-seq samples; GSE207400 matrix values are already log-normalised
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx: Clinical data, 108 samples, 15 columns (Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq, etc.)
   - GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz: Bulk RNA-seq log-normalised matrix, 14,995 genes x 48 samples (49 columns including GeneID)
   - Methylation groups represented in bulk RNA-seq: MPNST_G1 (n=6), MPNST_G2 (n=6), PremalignantNF_G3 (n=18), Benign_NF-G4 (n=9), Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 (n=9). Note: Cutaneous NF_G6 and Cutaneous NF_G7 have NO bulk RNA-seq samples.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: Samples with bulk RNA-seq data -> 48 samples (clinical sample M1933 with 'X' for RNA-Seq is missing from matrix)
   - Filter 2: Samples with Methylation_Group assigned -> 48 samples
   - Final analytic cohort: 48 samples across 5 methylation subgroups (no G6/G7 bulk data available)

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Methylation_Group: categorical from clinical Excel (values: MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2, PremalignantNF_G3, Benign_NF-G4, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5)
   - Non-syndromic spinal NF = the 'Sporadic Spinal NF_G5' methylation subgroup (per paper nomenclature)

D. COMPUTATION
   - BACKEND NOTE: The paper's Supp Fig 13e uses CIBERSORTx with a custom signature matrix derived from the authors' own snRNA-seq data. That custom signature matrix is NOT included in the available supplementary data (GEO/Supp Tables contain fusion, mutation, DE gene, drug-screen, clinical data but NO CIBERSORTx signature/fractions). The snRNA-seq at GSE207399 has only 6 samples (5 MPNST + 1 benign NF), with NO sporadic spinal NF samples - the paper's signature was likely derived from external/unavailable data.
   - Unable to exactly reproduce CIBERSORTx deconvolution. Performed marker-based approximation as specified in the question prompt.
   - Approximation approach #1 (canonical markers): Compute a module score using 19 canonical Schwann-cell/peripheral-nerve markers (S100B, MPZ, PMP22, NGFR, GFAP, PMP2, MBP, CDH19, ERBB3, L1CAM, NRXN1, SCN7A, ITGB8, PLP1, EGR2, MAG, PRX, NCAM1, CRYAB). For each marker, z-scored expression across all 48 samples; mean z-score per sample = 'NF Cell Type' score; aggregated by methylation subgroup.
   - Approximation approach #2 (data-driven markers): Identified top 50 genes enriched in Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 vs all others (logFC >= 2, expressed above 25th percentile). Top markers (SLITRK1, SCN7A, COL28A1, NRXN1, GABRG1, CADM2, CYP4B1, PMP2, CDH19, CHL1) are classical peripheral nerve/Schwann-cell transcripts, consistent with a 'non-syndromic spinal NF' signature. Same z-score/module-score approach applied.

E. RESULT
   Approach #1 (canonical NF/Schwann markers) — mean NF score by methylation group:
     Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 : 0.501  (n=9) [HIGHEST]
     PremalignantNF_G3     : 0.230  (n=18)
     Benign_NF-G4          : 0.186  (n=9)
     MPNST_G2              : -0.409 (n=6)
     MPNST_G1              : -1.313 (n=6)

   Approach #2 (data-driven G5-specific markers) — mean NF score by methylation group:
     Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 : 0.804  (n=9) [HIGHEST]
     Benign_NF-G4          : 0.257  (n=9)
     PremalignantNF_G3     : 0.098  (n=18)
     MPNST_G2              : -0.830 (n=6)
     MPNST_G1              : -1.056 (n=6)

   Both approaches agree: the Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 (non-syndromic spinal NF) methylation subgroup has the highest CIBERSORTx-like 'NF Cell Type' score in bulk samples. This is biologically expected - the non-syndromic spinal NF cell-type signature should be maximally abundant in tumors of the sporadic spinal NF methylation subgroup.

F. CAVEATS
   - The paper's exact CIBERSORTx signature matrix is unavailable, so this is an approximation using marker-based module scoring rather than true deconvolution.
   - Only 5 of the 7 methylation subgroups have bulk RNA-seq samples (Cutaneous NF_G6 and Cutaneous NF_G7 have zero bulk samples), so the comparison is limited to those 5 groups.
   - Approach #2 has potential circularity (markers derived from G5 then used to score G5), but approach #1 uses external canonical markers and gives the same ranking, supporting the conclusion.
   - Results are ordinal (ranking) only - absolute magnitudes are not comparable to CIBERSORTx fractions.
